claim: Lane: synthesis-leaning research: Gen 1/2 AI behavior and exploits, modern Pokemon AI heuristics, ROM-constrained heuristic AI patterns, prior boss-AI work via git log --grep=boss | Intended outcome: Post one slice_update with 5-10 candidate smartness levers, each carrying current-behavior cite, smarter behavior, public-info-only check, ROM cost estimate, WRAMX cost estimate, and play-impact estimate.

extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: add codex research lane findings
files_about_to_read: web sources for vanilla Gen 1/2 AI and modern Pokemon AI

claim: Codex synthesis lane found eight ROM-expansion smartness levers. The strongest direction is table-driven public estimators plus bounded reply-bucket planning, not broad hidden-state tree search. Empty ROMX banks are the right substrate; WRAMX use should stay zero or tiny until Claude verifies bank 2-7 availability.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Public damage and KO-band oracle
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: BossAI_CurrentEnemyMoveHasKOPressure and BossAI_CurrentEnemyMovePressureScore are coarse pressure estimators at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:2977 and :3011; ScaleEnemyMovePowerByAttackStat is base-stat-ratio only at :3139-3146; public speed is separate at :3456.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Build ROMX tables/routines that estimate public damage bands, 2HKO/3HKO windows, survival bands, and deny-KO odds from visible species/level/typing/HP/status/stages, boss-known own moves/items, revealed player moves, and observed damage calibration.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Do not read wBattleMon private stats, hidden moves, hidden items, unseen party, current-turn input, or future RNG. Player-side stat uncertainty becomes coarse banding or observed-damage refinement only. Boss own party/item facts are legal.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 1-2 ROMX banks for tables plus dispatch; more if type-passive and held-item public modifiers are fully tableized.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0 bytes if recomputed per candidate; 8-16 bytes scratch/cache only if Claude verifies WRAMX bank 2-7 availability. Do not spend bank-1 trace reserve.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: very high. Improves KO, deny-KO, recovery, setup affordability, sacrifice cash-out, and switch confidence across every boss without making them omniscient.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Coach-plan template engine in ROM
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: current plan bias is small direct scoring in BossAI_ApplyPlanMoveBias at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:4702; current lookahead is top-candidate local evaluation at :4818-4937; offline Coach Mode already has 3-5 turn plan cards in tools/boss_ai_preference/README.md:15-31 and plan_queue scenarios in audit/boss_ai_preference/plan_queue.md:12-60.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Compile a compact ROMX table of public-gated plan templates: attack_now, setup_once_then_attack, sleep_then_setup, pressure_recover_then_lock, scout_probe_then_commit, switch_preserve_then_rescore, hazard_phaze_route, and cashout_sacrifice. Plan identity supplies action-sequence bias and stop conditions rather than a literal tree.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Templates can inspect legal boss actions, boss roster, visible field/HP/status/boosts, seen species, revealed moves, public plausible masks, and observed switch history. They must keep fixture-style separation of revealed/plausible/impossible/unknown and never treat unrevealed player moves as facts.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 2-3 ROMX banks if implemented as data-heavy templates plus small dispatcher; 1 bank for a minimal first subset.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 8-24 bytes for active template id/phase/target/confidence/stop flags if persistent; 0-4 bytes for one-turn stateless scoring variant.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: very high for named fights. Directly imports existing human-labeled multi-turn lessons into ROM behavior, especially Whitney, Lance, Bugsy, Clair, Koga, Jasmine, and Red.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Public tendency counters beyond switch count
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: BossAI_PredictPlayerSwitch is mostly switch count, turn count, HP band, public threat, and revealed SE at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:3531; pending current-turn switch input commits next turn through BossAI_IncrementTurnsElapsed and BossAI_RecordPlayerSwitch at engine/battle/ai/boss_platform.asm:24 and :75.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Add tiny public tendency counters for behaviors already observed after resolution: switches under threat, attacks into bad public matchup, protects/recovers repeatedly, greedy setup under pressure, status fishing, and low-HP sack acceptance. Feed them into reply-bucket weights and plan confidence.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Only update from visible resolved actions, never from current menu/input. Current-turn switch remains pending until the next turn commit point, preserving the no-hidden-info timing boundary.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: about 0.5-1 ROMX bank for update/scoring tables.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 6-16 bytes if multiple 2-bit counters are kept; a strict first slice could use one 2-bit tendency and stay inside proven reserve only after memory audit.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high after turn 3. Makes rematches, Elite Four, Champion, and Red feel adaptive without deterministic counterpicking.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Public role/package classifier for seen player species
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: platform tracks seen species and per-species revealed move memory at engine/battle/ai/boss_platform.asm:209-360; public threat masks use visible species, revealed moves, learnability, and known type data per engine/battle/ai/PLATFORM_API.md:54-64; plausible masks are built in boss_policy_move around :4555.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Classify each seen player species into coarse public packages: spinner, phazer, setup sweeper, recovery wall, priority revenge, sleep/status pressure, trap/perish line, physical/special wallbreaker. Use package bits for preservation switches, plan templates, and route valuation.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Inputs are visible species, public learnability, revealed moves, visible behavior, and observed role evidence. Output must remain probabilistic/package-level and cannot imply exact hidden moves or exact four-slot sets.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 1-2 ROMX banks for species/effect package tables plus classifier; less if table only covers boss-relevant species/effects.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0 bytes if recomputed for active/seen candidates; 6-12 bytes if caching package bits for six seen species after WRAMX verification.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high for veteran counterplay. Bosses stop treating every public type threat identically and start respecting roles such as spinner, setup, revenge, and wall without cheating.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Table-driven revealed-effect interaction matrix
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: current move policy has many bespoke revealed-effect helpers such as Protect/Recovery/Encore/Selfdestruct/SleepPreempt calls around engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:1392-1605; POLICY_DESIGN.md:49-59 says the intended shape is pressure + plan + coverage/denial + lookahead + pattern - risks.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Move the growing bespoke effect interactions into ROMX data keyed by revealed player effect, boss candidate effect/category, tier, public speed/HP gates, and board flags. Include Destiny Bond, Counter/Mirror Coat, Protect, Encore, recovery, anti-setup, Disable, Mean Look/Nightmare/Perish, charging/rampage, phaze/hazard, and sacrifice-move interactions.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Only exact active revealed moves in wPlayerUsedMoves/per-species public memory, public last move, visible HP/status/boosts, public speed predicate, and boss candidate move data. No plausible hidden move becomes an exact revealed-effect trigger.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 1 ROMX bank for dense table plus small dispatcher; could replace code growth in tight bank 0e.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high and structurally safe. Expands tactical coverage while reducing scattered bespoke ASM drift and making audits easier.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Top-K reply-bucket payoff matrix with mixed near-tie policy
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: docs/boss_ai_spec.md:290-335 already specifies 4 strategic candidates and 3 reply buckets; BossAI_SelectMove currently chooses best vs second with 90/75/60 odds after lookahead at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:2611-2763; docs/boss_ai_lessons_implementation_plan_2026-05-14.md:139-146 warns broad bucket tuning should follow measurement.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: For late bosses only, evaluate top 3-4 legal boss actions against three public reply buckets: stay/attack, preserve/switch, greed/setup. Use public payoff tables and controlled roulette/softmax to mix close good plays, choose forcing lines when dominant, and avoid deterministic memorization.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Reply buckets come from public HP/status/boosts, revealed/plausible masks, observed tendencies, seen species, and prior turns. It must not condition on the player's current selected button or private party/moves/items.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 1-2 ROMX banks if payoff tables include plan/personality/tier variants; 0.5 bank for a minimal matrix over existing scores.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0-12 bytes scratch for 4x3 scores; no persistent state required.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high for champion/endgame and near-tie cases, medium for early bosses. This is the ROM-friendly adaptation of modern MCTS/minimax lessons under hardware constraints.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Role-aware boss party resource and preservation table
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: switch flow and confidence are in BossAI_SwitchOrTryItem/BossAI_ComputeSwitchConfidence at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_switch.asm:17 and :685; switch candidate refinement is risk-focused at :765; preservation bias exists at BossAI_ApplyPreservationSwitchBias :1242; lessons plan names preservation and cash-out refinement as future candidates at docs/boss_ai_lessons_implementation_plan_2026-05-14.md:518-548.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Add per-trainer/per-mon role values for ace, cleaner, hazard setter, spinner, status piece, wallbreaker, pivot, sack, and route-critical piece. Switch and sacrifice scoring uses remaining utility, public threat, boss phase, and plan identity to preserve or cash out the right piece.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Boss may know its own party and role plan. Player-side risk remains public: visible active, seen species, revealed moves, public plausible masks, hazards, HP bands, and observed tendencies only.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 0.5-1.5 ROMX banks depending on table density and trainer coverage.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0-4 bytes if current role can be looked up from trainer/slot; no persistent player memory required.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high for fights with utility mons and aces. Prevents wasteful sacks, pointless setup turns, and losing route-critical pieces while still allowing dramatic cash-outs.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Observed speed and damage calibration
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: current public speed compares base speed plus known enemy Scarf at engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:3456; current pressure scales by base stat ratio at :3139-3146; PLATFORM_API public memory currently covers seen species/revealed moves/turn/switch counts but not observed damage-speed bands at engine/battle/ai/PLATFORM_API.md:25-35.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: After turns resolve, record public observations such as who moved first, approximate damage band dealt/received, crit-known where visible, and whether a predicted KO band missed. Use this to refine future speed, KO, recovery, and switch estimates for the current encounter.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Only post-resolution facts both players could observe. No current-turn input, hidden stats/items, exact IV/EV, or hidden reserves. Updates happen at the same next-turn boundary as pending switch observations.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: about 1 ROMX bank for update, compression, and consumers.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 8-24 bytes if keeping active-pair calibration; 0 if reduced to one encounter-level speed relation and one damage bucket. Needs Claude WRAMX verification before persistence.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: medium-high, strongest in longer boss fights. Lets bosses learn from public battle evidence the way a human would and reduces repeated bad base-stat assumptions.

repo_evidence: repo-proven: boss AI entrypoints and current anchors are indexed in docs/generated/dev_index.md:28-31.

repo-proven: ROMX has 943063 free bytes and many full empty 16 KiB banks in docs/generated/dev_index.md:73-139; boss WRAM reserve in bank 1 has only 36 normal bytes / 9 trace bytes in docs/generated/dev_index.md:84-91.
repo-proven: ordinary boss policy must stay public-info-only per engine/battle/ai/POLICY_DESIGN.md:31-42 and engine/battle/ai/PLATFORM_API.md:33-64.
repo-proven: current design already expects raw buttons, few strategic candidates, and 3 reply buckets rather than broad literal search in docs/boss_ai_spec.md:290-335.
repo-proven: current move model already covers many tactical rules but selection is best-vs-second after bounded lookahead, per docs/boss_ai_lessons_implementation_plan_2026-05-14.md:122-166.
repo-proven: prior boss work exists via git log --grep=boss, including 09b6c8ea self-ko/spikes tuning and the boss-ai-debugger closed-loop tooling series.
phase_a2_ranking_suggestion: judgment: Prioritize levers 1, 2, 5, 6, and 7 for convergence because they spend ROMX, can be audited, and directly support the roadmap's structural-smartness target.
judgment: Treat levers 3 and 8 as WRAMX-dependent stretch work until Claude's bank 2-7 verification lands; do not spend bank-1 reserve casually.
judgment: Use lever 4 as a support table for levers 2, 6, and 7 rather than a standalone first implementation if phase budget gets tight.
extra.next_step: Await Claude's P0_brainstorm_claude_research_lane slice_update, then run Phase A.2 adversarial ranking. No implementation commit or roadmap lock until mutual slice_review/mutual_done rows exist.

claim: Lane: analysis-leaning research: current boss AI architecture limits (engine/battle/ai/boss*.asm), ROM bank inventory verification, WRAMX bank 2-7 availability check, public-info corpus enumeration, plus addressing the 2 contract drift concerns Codex flagged in P0_brainstorm_codex_research_lane | Intended outcome: Post one slice_update with: (a) 5-10 candidate smartness levers in the 6-field shape (current-behavior cite, smarter behavior, public-info-only check, ROM cost, WRAMX cost, play-impact); (b) repo-proven WRAMX bank 2-7 availability verdict; (c) ROM bank inventory summary with explicit free-byte numbers; (d) public-info corpus enumeration; (e) status on the 2 drift concerns Codex flagged (verify presence on debugger branch + propose restore plan if absent).

extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: add claude research lane findings + contract drift resolution

claim: Claude analysis lane confirms ROM is ample (14 fully empty ROMX banks + partial = ~229KB+ free), WRAMX banks 2-7 are completely undeclared (free but bank-switching plumbing needs extension), and identifies 6 complementary smartness levers focused on per-leader specialization, Haki structuring, and observation tooling. Both Codex contract drift concerns are repo-proven: docs/llm_pairing_rules.md and tools/audit/check_two_llm_handoff_log.py exist on claude/debugger-masterpiece-roadmap (commits 0d3fbf8c and da2f6644) but are absent on this branch; restore via cherry-pick + adding to no_solo_commits SETUP_ALLOWLIST as Phase A.0.5 work.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Per-leader personality kernels in ROMX data
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: POLICY_DESIGN.md:64-72 documents tier-based defaults (early simple/bold, mid respects revealed SE, late preserves high-value mons, champion explainable adaptation) but tier maps to a few enums; per-leader behavior is mostly hand-authored asm with no externalized weights or selection mechanisms.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Externalize scoring-weight vectors + plan-template preferences + Haki triggers into ROMX kernel data, keyed by trainer ID (one kernel per major fight; tier-default fallback). At battle start, load the kernel into scratch (existing wBossAI* or new WRAMX-2). Each major leader gets a curated personality (Falkner risk-aversive flying-pivot, Whitney stubborn-Miltank, Jasmine Steelix-protective, Lance chain-Dragonite-pressure, Karen mixed-Dark, Red elite-balanced-ace).
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Kernels are compile-time constants per leader; no hidden-info reads. Each kernel weights public-info inputs only.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 1-3 ROMX banks (one per ~6-10 leaders dense; 1 minimal for tier-template subset). Fits in any of the 14 empty banks.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0 bytes if loaded into existing wBossAI scratch on battle start; up to 32 bytes in WRAMX-2 if richer state needed.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: very high. Serves First-Playthrough Promise directly. Complementary to Codex #2 (Coach-plan templates which is fight-shape); this is trainer-character.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Trainer-specific Haki playbook tables
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: judgment: CLAUDE.md North Star sanctions Haki as 'explicitly authored once-per-battle exceptions only', but current implementation lives in hand-coded asm scattered around scoring with no central data structure. Hard to audit, easy to drift.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: ROMX table keyed by trainer ID listing trigger conditions (e.g. HP < 30%, lost an ace, turn > 6 + resolved public threat) and authored once-per-battle exception action (specific move, specific switch, specific Haki bias). Centralized so Cole + audits can review the full set.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Triggers are public-info-only (HP band, ace KO state, turn counter, observed-tendency counters). Action is pre-authored, not computed from hidden info.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 0.5-1 ROMX bank for full leader coverage + dispatch.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 1-2 bytes (Haki-used-this-battle flag + selected-trigger flag) within existing reserve.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: very high for fight texture. Players come to recognize and dread 'the moment Leader X pulls their card.' Currently hard to express; with this structure, designable. Codex did not cover Haki.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Compile-time boss-roster type-matchup precompute tables
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: PLATFORM_API.md mentions Public Move And Type Helpers + Public Threat Model but runtime type lookups happen via the type chart for every move evaluation every turn. Per-boss roster, the same matchups are computed thousands of times across a playthrough.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: For each boss authored roster, precompute at build time: defensive matchup vector per slot vs all 17 types; offensive coverage vectors per slot. Store in ROMX as a per-trainer table; lookup at slot selection replaces type-chart loops.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Roster is boss own (legal); type chart is public; matchups derived are public.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 0.5-1 ROMX bank if all major leaders covered.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: medium for direct behavior (frees cycle budget), high for enabling Codex #1 (KO-band oracle) and #6 (reply buckets) which get matchup data free.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Structured ROM-side post-turn observation log (refinement of Codex #8)
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: BossAI_RecordPlayerSwitch at boss_platform.asm:75 records switch resolutions; revealed moves tracked at boss_platform.asm:209-360. But no structured per-encounter observation buffer of (turn, action, outcome, damage_band, speed_known) for later AI consultation.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Reserve a small WRAMX-2 buffer (16-32 bytes) for the last ~6 turns of structured public observations: turn_no, actor (boss/player), action_class, observed_damage_band, observed_speed_relation. Append on resolution; consult during scoring for 'did the player Roar last turn? skip the trap line' or 'player damage to me was higher than my band predicted, refine'.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: All entries are public post-resolution facts. No current-turn input, no hidden stat read.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 0.5 ROMX bank for append + consult helpers.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: repo-proven: 16-32 bytes; fits within proposed WRAMX-2 bank carve. Cannot fit in existing 36/9-byte bank-1 reserve.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high. Complements Codex #3 (tendency counters) and #8 (calibration); my version is the structured underlying buffer those would read from.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Decoupled Haki action slot with signal text
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: judgment: existing Haki is 'boss does action X on trigger Y' without any in-game signal that something special happened. The player cannot reliably learn 'Leader Z has a Haki card.' Currently invisible learning loop.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: When Haki fires, set a flag that surfaces a subtle but consistent text variant ('Leader Z stays focused!' / 'Leader Z waits for the right moment.') designable per-leader. Cole-taste: discoverable signal without explicit 'I cheated' framing. Combined with #2 (Haki playbook tables), the player gradually learns each leader tells.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Trigger conditions remain public-info (per #2); the signal is post-resolution text, not new hidden info exposed.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: 0.25 ROMX bank for text variants + trigger plumbing.
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: judgment: 0-1 bytes (Haki-fired flag from #2 already exists).
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: high for First-Playthrough Promise specifically. Gives learnable 'I can read this leader' loop, the kind of mastery that survives veteran-knowledge attrition. Cole gameplay-taste item.

candidate_smartness_levers.name: Public RNG transparency surface for boss_ai_debugger
candidate_smartness_levers.current_behavior_cite: repo-proven: BossAI_SelectMove uses 90/75/60 odds for best/second/third per engine/battle/ai/boss_policy_move.asm:2611-2763. The dice outcome is consumed but not exposed to the trace surface. The boss_ai_debugger tooling (a32c4958 + 933c76c3) reads scores but cannot easily replay the random selection.
candidate_smartness_levers.smarter_behavior: Add a single byte to existing boss-AI trace fields recording which candidate was rolled (1st/2nd/3rd) plus the dice value. Lets boss_ai_debugger replay the exact selection. Pure tooling improvement; no gameplay change.
candidate_smartness_levers.public_info_only_check: Trace surface is debugger-only, never exposed to runtime AI. No hidden-info leakage to either side.
candidate_smartness_levers.rom_cost_estimate: judgment: <0.1 ROMX bank (a few bytes of additional trace write).
candidate_smartness_levers.wramx_cost_estimate: repo-proven: 1-2 trace bytes; fits within existing BOSS_AI_TRACE 9-byte reserve.
candidate_smartness_levers.play_impact_estimate: judgment: low for player; high for development velocity. Pair can audit 'why did the boss pick the second-best move' faster, reducing iteration time on later levers.

repo_evidence: repo-proven: 14 fully empty ROMX banks at $4000-$7fff (16384 bytes each): 13, 22, 27, 28, 29, 2c, 2d, 2f, 34, 35, 58, 63, 67, 6f. dev_index.md 'Largest ROMX Free Ranges' section confirms.
repo-proven: ROMX has 943063 total free bytes per dev_index.md.
repo-proven: Tight banks (0 free): ROMX 12/15/17/1b/1e, WRAMX 01. Adding there is forbidden without relocation.
repo-proven: Boss AI WRAM Reserve in dev_index.md shows wBossAITier at 01:d72b, wBossAIStateEnd at 01:d793; normal 104 used / 36 free; trace 131 used / 9 free.

repo-proven: ram/wram.asm lines 1740/2223/2647/2792 declare WRAMX SECTIONs without BANK[N] pins; linker packs all into bank 1 (dev_index Tight Banks shows WRAMX 01 saturated). No SECTION pins to banks 2-7.
repo-proven: docs/llm_pairing_rules.md exists on claude/debugger-masterpiece-roadmap at commit 0d3fbf8c; absent on claude/boss-ai-rom-expansion.
repo-proven: tools/audit/check_two_llm_handoff_log.py exists on claude/debugger-masterpiece-roadmap at commit da2f6644; absent on claude/boss-ai-rom-expansion. v7 strict cannot pass until restored.

repo-proven: POLICY_DESIGN.md:31-42 is the canonical public-info policy contract; POLICY_DESIGN.md:13-23 lists existing helpers (BossAI_ComputePlayerPlausibleTypeMask, BossAI_SelectPlanIfNeeded, BossAI_ApplyLookaheadToTopMoveCandidates, BossAI_SelectMove, BossAI_PredictPlayerSwitch, BossAI_ApplyRepeatPenalty, BossAI_RefineSwitchCandidateForPlausibleRisk).
repo-proven: PLATFORM_API.md has sections: Entry Gates, Platform State Tracking, Public Move And Type Helpers, Public Threat Model, Policy Surface, Trace Surface.

repo-proven: prior boss-AI work includes 09b6c8ea (self-ko/spikes tuning), 7b368233 (score-rule materialization), 933c76c3 (boss trace proof corpus), 11e8cdf8 (TD-004 boss.asm split), a32c4958 (closed-loop debugger tooling).
public_info_corpus_enumeration: visible_field_effects (weather, screens, hazards, sub presence, trap state)
observed_resolved_actions (post-turn: who-moved, switch/attack chosen, damage band)
public_info_excluded_list_repo_proven: wBattleMon* private stats beyond visible (exact IVs, EVs, exact computed stats)
hidden_items (player held items not yet revealed by use)
unseen_party_slots (party members not yet sent out)

wramx_bank_2_7_availability_verdict.evidence: repo-proven: ram/wram.asm lines 1740/2223/2647/2792 declare SECTIONs as WRAMX without BANK[N] pins. Linker packs them all into bank 1 (dev_index Tight Banks confirms WRAMX 01 saturated at 0 free bytes; no mention of banks 2-7 in dev_index).
plumbing_requirements: Declare new SECTIONs with explicit BANK[02] / BANK[03] / etc constraints in ram/wram.asm (additive, no existing layout disturbed).
Boss AI bank-switch code already handles WRAMX bank 1; extend to bank 2-7 for new state access (existing hROMBank shadow + save/restore idiom per CLAUDE.md RAM rules).
No SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION bump required (WRAMX is volatile, not SRAM-backed).
wramx_bank_2_7_availability_verdict.recommended_use: judgment: bank 2 for structured observation log (lever #4) + Codex #8 calibration; bank 3 reserved for plan-state if Codex #2 templates need persistence; banks 4-7 unallocated for now.

absent_files_repo_proven: tools/audit/check_two_llm_handoff_log.py (last seen commit da2f6644 on claude/debugger-masterpiece-roadmap)
contract_drift_resolution_proposal.restore_plan: Phase A.0.5 setup-allowlist commit: cherry-pick docs/llm_pairing_rules.md + tools/audit/check_two_llm_handoff_log.py from claude/debugger-masterpiece-roadmap. Update tools/audit/check_no_solo_commits_boss_ai_rom_expansion.py SETUP_ALLOWLIST to include both. Verify v7 strict on the empty handoff log passes. judgment: the softmax/roulette distribution spec is load-bearing. Too sharp -> deterministic memorizable (veteran-knowledge exploit). Too smooth -> pure randomness (no skill expression). Recommend: tie the distribution temperature into the per-leader personality kernel (Claude #1) so each leader has a curated softmax — Falkner sharper (less mixing), Karen smoother (more mixing). This is a Phase A.2 design note, not a research-row blocker.

claim: Claude opens P0.5b WRAMX bank-2 plumbing. SCOPE CORRECTION upfront: the locked roadmap said 'extend the existing bank-switch idiom' but repo-proven search shows rSVBK is defined in constants/hardware.inc:1108 and used NOWHERE in the working tree (.asm / .inc). The boss AI bank handling references wBossAITier etc. but those all live in default WRAMX bank 1; no bank-switch code exists for WRAMX. So P0.5b is BUILDING the idiom, not extending one. Sending this ack_start + a chat ack-request to Codex before any commit (per the 'new phases need chat ack first' rule from feedback_codex_commit_then_review_rule).
write_set: ram/hram.asm or constants/wram.inc (new hWRAMBank HRAM shadow byte)
home/ new helper file OR home/init.asm extension (SetWRAMBank helper + initial SVBK=1)
collision_risk_files: ram/wram.asm (potential conflict if Codex also touches it)
files_read: docs/generated/dev_index.md (Boss AI WRAM Reserve + Tight Banks confirming WRAMX 01 saturated)

design_proposal_v1.section_declaration: ram/wram.asm: add SECTION 'Boss AI WRAMX2 Buffer', WRAMX, BANK[2]. Reserve a single placeholder label wBossAIWramx2Buffer:: db ; (1 byte placeholder so SECTION is non-empty). Future P5 (observation log cluster) populates the actual buffer.
design_proposal_v1.hwrambank_shadow: Add hWRAMBank to HRAM (1 byte) following the hROMBank shadow pattern. Initial value 1 (default WRAMX bank). Track current SVBK in this shadow so callers can save/restore without re-reading the IO register.
design_proposal_v1.set_wram_bank_helper: Add SetWRAMBank:: in home/ taking the target bank in 'a'. Writes to [rSVBK] then [hWRAMBank]. Caller's responsibility to save/restore 'a' if they want it preserved. Recommend usage pattern: push current shadow value, set target, do work, pop+restore. Inline-able for hot paths; helper preferred for clarity. No DI/EI needed (interrupts don't touch WRAMX-1-or-2 data structures in this hack).
design_proposal_v1.boot_initialization: Verify the boot init path sets SVBK=1 explicitly. If not, add it to home/init.asm or wherever HRAM is zero'd. Defensive: post-condition for any boot path is [rSVBK]=1, [hWRAMBank]=1.
design_proposal_v1.audit_script: tools/audit/check_wramx_bank2_declaration.py: verifies the SECTION with BANK[2] exists in ram/wram.asm; verifies the boot-init path sets hWRAMBank=1; verifies dev_index regen lists WRAMX bank 2 with non-zero used bytes; verifies bank 1 reserve (BOSS_AI_TRACE 9-byte floor) is unchanged.

extra.acceptance_criterion_check: Per locked roadmap: 'build green; make compare may diverge (new SECTION is fine, no parity claim); dev_index.md regenerated showing WRAMX bank 02 used bytes > 0; existing Boss AI WRAM Reserve table still shows >=9 trace bytes free in bank 1.'
extra.open_question_for_codex: (1) Inline pattern vs helper function for the bank-switch idiom — which does Codex prefer as the canonical idiom going forward? (2) Should hWRAMBank live in existing hram.asm or a new file? (3) Boot init: does any existing path already set SVBK=1 explicitly, or do we need to add it? (4) Is there a preferred home/ file for the helper, or do we create a new wram_bank.asm? (5) Should SetWRAMBank disable interrupts defensively (di/ei) for future-proofing even though current interrupts don't touch WRAMX 1-or-2?
extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: P0.5b WRAMX bank-2 plumbing - new SECTION + SetWRAMBank helper + hWRAMBank shadow + audit
extra.next_step: Send chat message to Codex with: (a) the rSVBK-not-used finding (scope correction), (b) this design proposal, (c) the 5 open questions. Wait for Codex chat ack before implementing. After Codex green-lights design, I commit + Codex slice_reviews.

claim: P0.5b shipped at commit 92bd7260 incorporating all 3 Codex chat-ack corrections + the 1 bonus audit suggestion. 8 files changed: ram/wram.asm new BANK[2] SECTION with wBossAIWramx2Buffer placeholder; ram/hram.asm converted unlabeled ds 1 to hWRAMBank (no net HRAM growth); home/wram_bank.asm new SetWRAMBank helper (no di/ei per Codex correction); home.asm includes wram_bank next to sram.asm (hardware-banking neighborhood); home/init.asm hCGB-guarded [rSVBK]=1 before WRAM clear + [hWRAMBank]=1 after HRAM clear; tools/audit/check_wramx_bank2_declaration.py new (8 checks: SECTION pin, shadow byte, helper, include, init writes, hCGB guard, bank-1 reserve floor, sym resolution); tools/audit/check_rsvbk_write_discipline.py new (Codex bonus audit: rSVBK writes confined to home/init.asm + home/wram_bank.asm OR paired with [hWRAMBank] write within 3 lines); docs/generated/dev_index.md regenerated. ROM builds, links, wBossAIWramx2Buffer resolves at 02:d000, Boss AI WRAM Reserve bank-1 floor unchanged (Normal 104/36 free, Trace 131/9 free).

design_corrections_applied_from_codex_ch: (1) Helper canonical: SetWRAMBank for normal sites; direct ldh [rSVBK] only in boot init.
(2) hWRAMBank repurposed an unlabeled ds 1 in ram/hram.asm, no net HRAM growth (HRAM is full per dev_index).
(3) Boot init: hCGB-guarded [rSVBK]=1 before WRAM clear; [hWRAMBank]=1 after HRAM clear (since HRAM clear would zero the shadow).
(4) home/wram_bank.asm included next to home/sram.asm (hardware-banking, not copy/array).
(5) No di/ei inside SetWRAMBank (Codex: helper can't safely restore IME + only protects 2 writes; future bank-2 access sites bracket if needed).
(BONUS) Added check_rsvbk_write_discipline.py audit catching undisciplined inline [rSVBK] writes.
extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: P0.5b WRAMX bank-2 plumbing - SECTION + SetWRAMBank + hWRAMBank shadow + audits
extra.next_step: Codex slice_review of commit 92bd7260 with status=slice_accepted (closes P0_5b_wramx_bank2_plumbing) or slice_revisions_requested with specific edits. After acceptance, v7 strict goes fully green; P0.5b is the first non-setup-allowlist paired slice mutual-verified on this branch.

claim: Reviewed P0.5b commit 92bd7260. Status: slice_accepted. The implementation matches the chat-acked design corrections: hWRAMBank uses an existing HRAM placeholder with no net HRAM growth; SetWRAMBank is a HOME helper with no di/ei and keeps rSVBK/hWRAMBank in sync; boot init sets rSVBK=1 before the WRAM clear and hWRAMBank=1 after HRAM clear; the BANK[2] section resolves wBossAIWramx2Buffer at 02:d000; the bonus rSVBK write-discipline audit catches shadow-desync write sites. Binary check of pokegold.gbc at Init confirms the WRAM clear still loads bc=$2000, so adding bank 2 did not expand the startup clear loop past WRAM0 plus one selected WRAMX bank. PAIR_WINDOW=3 is accepted as the inline-switch escape hatch because it enforces hardware-write then shadow-write locality without blessing broader manual banking. Non-blocking future note: P5 must explicitly initialize/clear any real bank-2 observation buffer before first use; P0.5b only declares the placeholder and switching idiom.

accepted_audit_thresholds.reason: Small enough to catch shadow desync, large enough for disciplined inline write pairs if a future hot path needs them.

claim: Claude opens P0.5c haki-audit tool stub. Tools-only slice extending tools/boss_ai_debugger/ with a new haki-coverage subcommand that parses the Haki Per-Leader Roster from docs/boss_ai_spec.md (the 19-leader table at :66-84) and surfaces it for audit/balance review. Reads existing spec; no new data structure. ROM cost 0, WRAMX cost 0. Acceptance per locked roadmap P0.5c: 'python -m tools.boss_ai_debugger haki-coverage --self-test exits 0 and reports all 19 Haki leaders'.
safe_write_set_for_other: all asm files (no asm in this slice)

extra.design_decision: Per-leader Haki entries are descriptive in spec but load-bearing for audit clarity. Tool parses the markdown table, validates 19/19 against EXPECTED_LEADERS_19 constant, surfaces a formatted listing. No cross-check yet with data/trainers/parties.asm — that's a future expansion. Minimal viable v1 ships the parser + self-test only.
extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: P0.5c haki-audit tool stub - haki-coverage subcommand + audit

claim: P0.5c shipped. tools/boss_ai_debugger/haki_coverage.py parses the Haki Per-Leader Roster from docs/boss_ai_spec.md (regex-matched table rows, skips header + separator), exposes EXPECTED_LEADERS_19 constant (Morty, Chuck, Jasmine, Pryce, Clair, Will, Koga, Bruno, Karen, Lance, Brock, Misty, Lt. Surge, Erika, Janine, Sabrina, Blaine, Blue, Red), and provides run_haki_coverage() / format_haki_coverage() / run_self_test(). __main__.py wires the haki-coverage subcommand with --self-test and --json flags. tools/audit/check_haki_coverage_audit.py runs the self-test, checks exit code 0, parses for 'leaders: 19' line, fails on MISSING/EXTRA flags. Self-test output verified: 19/19 leaders listed correctly with ace species, level, iconic move per spec :66-84. Release-smoke stays green. tools-only — no ROM/WRAMX impact.
verification_replayed: python -m tools.boss_ai_debugger haki-coverage --self-test -> exit 0; lists 19/19 leaders

extra.intended_commit_message: boss-ai: P0.5c haki-audit tool stub - haki-coverage subcommand + audit
